place: Central Islip CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Central Islip station opened on November 4, 1873, replacing the earlier Suffolk station and officially naming the hamlet.
The hamlet was first called Germantown to attract German settlers but was renamed Islip Terrace during World War I because of German involvement.
The Central Islip Psychiatric Center, founded in 1889 as the New York City Farm for the Insane, became a cornerstone of the hamlet’s development.
Before European settlement, the Secatogue tribe—a subgroup of the Unkechaug people—inhabited the land that is now Central Islip.
The Long Island Rail Road’s arrival in 1842 established the Suffolk County Station, sparking agricultural growth and setting the stage for Central Islip’s expansion.
